metadata.title:
CLAIM PARK Plan IS


A COUNTRY CLUB GRAB

pages.raw_text:
That there is large and greedy
colored gentleman in the woodpile
is alleged by opponents of the
chamber of commences plan for
governing the city parks by self
perpetuating board named from
among it's members. Because of
this the opponents claim they are
lined up solidly and this is how
they explain their opposition:


The park board plan proposed, it
is claimed, came originally from H.
l.. Blocker, secretary and political
agent of the Washington Water
Power Co., E. F. Cartier Van Dis
sci, of the Sawmill Phoenix, and
l.. MacLean, a real estate man. 1t
was first proposed at bridge whist
game at the Spokane club, an or
ganization Of the wealthiest men
of Spokane That was about the
first week in May, 1905. The min
utes Of the chamber of commerce
show that it was endorsed and
Blocker, Van Dissel and MacLean
appointed a committee to draw up
a proposition. The minutes of the
chamber of commerce further show
it adopted the plan May 80, 1906.
The 10000 club was not there
mentioned. In order to work the
plan it was advertised as the ex-
